summary: USMCA looms over everything today. Sheinbaum pressed Trump and Carney at the World Cup final as a new round of trilateral talks opened in Mexico City, while officials pointed to a 23.2% drop in Asian imports as evidence Mexico has cut reliance on non-regional suppliers ahead of the review. Citi still expects the peso to weaken toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end. Mexico also remains absent from top destinations for private AI investment, unlike its USMCA partners.

lead.h1: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
lead.context: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.
lead.background: Banxico targets 3% inflation and sets its policy rate partly to keep Mexican rates above US levels, a differential that supports the peso and that investors watch closely.
lead.drivers: Citi's July survey points to depreciation as USMCA negotiations create uncertainty and analysts anticipate shifts in the Banxico-Fed rate gap.
lead.implications: The consensus implies the peso would give back part of its 2.86% first-half gain, closing 2026 near 17.92 pesos per dollar versus 17.49 at midyear.

coverage.title: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
coverage.summary: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.

items.headline: Sheinbaum presses USMCA talks with Trump and Carney at World Cup final
items.context: The brief in-person exchange coincided with the opening of a new round of trilateral trade talks in Mexico City on the treaty's review.
items.background: USMCA links Mexico, the US and Canada, and after the first joint review on 1 July 2026 the US declined a 16-year extension, so the pact now faces annual reviews under Article 34.7 through 2036.
items.drivers: Washington's new 50% tariff on Canadian goods, applied even to treaty-compliant products, is pushing Mexico to seek elimination of duties on its own rules-of-origin-compliant exports.
items.implications: The Canadian tariff, applied despite compliance with the treaty, shows Washington willing to tax partner goods even under USMCA rules.
items.next: Sheinbaum was preparing to host USTR Jamieson Greer in Mexico City as the new round of talks opened.

coverage.title: Sheinbaum presses USMCA talks with Trump and Carney at World Cup final
coverage.summary: The brief in-person exchange coincided with the opening of a new round of trilateral trade talks in Mexico City on the treaty's review.

items.headline: Officials report Asian imports fell 23.2% after Mexico’s tariffs
items.context: Officials say the drop in imports from Asia strengthens Mexico's position in the USMCA review by showing reduced reliance on non-regional suppliers.
items.background: In December 2025 Mexico imposed tariffs on imports from countries without a free trade agreement, aiming to curb the Chinese-origin components that flow through its factories despite USMCA rules of origin.
items.drivers: Between January and May, imports under those tariff lines from non-FTA countries fell 23.2% year-on-year, with China's drop at 28.4%, Taiwan's at 32.7% and South Korea's at 21.9%.
items.implications: Despite the broad decline, Chinese heavy trucks remain a separate obstacle to Mexico securing exemption from Trump's tariffs.

coverage.title: Officials report Asian imports fell 23.2% after Mexico’s tariffs
coverage.summary: Officials say the drop in imports from Asia strengthens Mexico's position in the USMCA review by showing reduced reliance on non-regional suppliers.

items.headline: Mexico is not among the leading destinations for private AI investment
items.context: The US and Canada rank among the top global recipients of private AI investment while Mexico does not appear on the list, despite all three being partners in the same trade pact.
items.background: Mexico, the US and Canada are USMCA partners, and Stanford's AI Index Report 2026 tracks which countries attract private capital for AI development as computing infrastructure needs grow.
items.drivers: The US led global private AI investment with $285,880 million in 2025, and Canada also ranked among the top five, while Mexico shows only a rise in graduates trained for technology development rather than investment.
items.implications: The index reported 173 data centers in Mexico at the end of 2025, compared with 337 in Canada and 5,427 in the US.
items.next: Watch new data-center projects and Mexico's position in the next AI Index to see whether the gap narrows.

coverage.title: Mexico is not among the leading destinations for private AI investment
coverage.summary: The US and Canada rank among the top global recipients of private AI investment while Mexico does not appear on the list, despite all three being partners in the same trade pact.

standing.text: The peso trades at 17.40 pesos to the dollar; inflation is 3.37%; the policy rate is 6.50%.
